behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names target file, LoRARequest import from vllm.lora.request, logger.info, lora_requests registry, app.state.openai_serving_models, and the helper signatures (serving_models/lora_name/lora_int_id and app/lora_name/lora_int_id/base_model_name).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes required behaviors (path inference rules, stale alias removal, success gating) without prescribing exact code or line-level changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use signature-based discovery (_has_required_args) and check behavioral outcomes (name in result, registry contents) rather than exact helper names or gold-specific tokens like 'xccl://'.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests exec extracted function sources in subprocess and call register_fn/infer_fn with mocks to verify runtime behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert concrete behavior like stale alias removal, path preservation, and name encoding in the fallback path, which a stub cannot satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover path inference (existing, same-id, fallback), registration (new LoRARequest, base_model_name), stale alias removal, and success gating is implied via ret_list processing.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped under comment section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass) and named per-behavior.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code behavior, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and identifiers consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the target file (which is the scope of the task) but does not give line numbers or specific buggy code snippets.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not a config edit.
